sources.name: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven

link.name: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven pg. 66
link.alt: Pathfinder #160: Assault on Hunting Lodge Seven pg. 66

stat_block.text: Used by gamblers and seers alike, this deck of cards comes in several varieties. Simple harrow decks are made from low-quality paper and typically have only an icon and a number to signify the suit and alignment. These simple decks are mostly used for games of chance, as the actual image and significance of the cards are irrelevant for such games. Common harrow decks are made from higher-quality paper and feature illustrations—harrow readers typically use these decks. Fine harrow decks are made from a variety of materials, such as high-quality paper, woods, bone, ivory, or metal.
